statement.normalized: Let $g(n)$ be minimal such that for any $A\subseteq [2,\infty)\cap \mathbb{N}$ with $\lvert A\rvert =n$ and any set $I$ of $\max(A)$ consecutive integers there exists some $B\subseteq I$ with $\lvert B\rvert=g(n)$ such that\[\prod_{a\in A} a \mid \prod_{b\in B}b.\]Is it true that\[g(n) \leq (2+o(1))n?\]Or perhaps even $g(n)\leq 2n$?
statement.original: Let $g(n)$ be minimal such that for any $A\subseteq [2,\infty)\cap \mathbb{N}$ with $\lvert A\rvert =n$ and any set $I$ of $\max(A)$ consecutive integers there exists some $B\subseteq I$ with $\lvert B\rvert=g(n)$ such that\[\prod_{a\in A} a \mid \prod_{b\in B}b.\]Is it true that\[g(n) \leq (2+o(1))n?\]Or perhaps even $g(n)\leq 2n$?
